request.description:
For /gaps endpoint there is a list of GET parameters which can influence the way of gaps definition.

Required parameters:

Employees

employees={employee_id1,employee_idN)

Size

size={1h30m}

Start date

startdate={YYYY-mm-dd}

End date

enddate={YYYY-mm-dd}

Optional parameters:

Postal code

postalcode={string}

Postal code different (radius)

postalode_diff={int}

Start time

starttime={H:i}

End time

endtime={H:i}

Rule

rule={0|1|2|3|4}
Rule parameter can take from 0 to 4 values which means:
  • 0 - default rule which is not alter current output from Timewave
  • 1 - sequantial rule to define gaps
  • 2 - parallel rule to define gaps
  • 3 - return the first free gap of the day
  • 4 - return the last free gap of the day

If rule equals 2 then interval between each gap can be defined (by default the interval is 15 minutes). For this purpose parameter "step" should be used. By default rule is 0.

Step

step={int}
This parameter is define the interval between each gap when parallel defining rule is used. Value should be integer in minutes. By default, it's 15 minutes.

item.name: Add extra service charge to workorderline

body.raw:
{
	"workorderline_id": 1796, // add a service to be charged together with existing workorderline
	"service_id": 79,
	"quantity": 12,
	"unit_price": 300, //Most often left out as you want default value to be charged
	"discount": 25, //Optional, Default value = 0, A value between 0 and 100. The value 100 equals 100 %.
	"description": "Service to be charged customer",
	"visible_invoice": true //can be used to hide the exact pricing of your services
}

item.name: Add outlay to employee on workorderline

body.raw:
{
	"workorderline_id": 1781, //mandatory
    "employee_id": 6, //mandatory
	"outlay_id": 1, //mandatory, found via looking at HTML on /supplements/outlays page in TimeWave interface
	"discount": 33.2, //optional, float beween 0 and 100
	"amount": 20000, //optional, the quantity of the outlay
	"price_per_unit": 20, //Optional, Default value = 0, A value between 0 and 100. The value 100 equals 100 %.
	"markup": 20, //float, min: 0, percentage, how much extra to bill the customer on top of cost?
	"to_invoice": 1, //boolean, is customer to be billed the cost?
    "outlay_by": "employee", //workarea or employee, if allowed to manipulate depend on system setting
	"description": "Bought important material"
}
